cases.name: allowed_public_read
cases.expected: resolver executes, no Retry-After header is attached, and one tenant-scoped trusted-IP key is consumed

cases.name: read_limit_exceeded
cases.expected: GraphQL error contains BLOG_RATE_LIMITED, surface, limit, and retryAfter extensions and the same retry value is attached as the Retry-After response header

cases.name: backend_unavailable
cases.expected: request fails closed with BLOG_RATE_LIMIT_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE and does not advertise Retry-After

cases.name: authorized_write
cases.expected: write limiter key uses authenticated user identity and operation surface and an exceeded result carries Retry-After

cases.name: unauthorized_write
cases.expected: module limiter is skipped so resolver authentication and RBAC remain authoritative without Retry-After

cases.name: moderation_write
cases.expected: blog_posts:manage maps to the dedicated moderate_comment surface

cases.name: memory_adapter_exceeded_mapping
cases.expected: the first request is allowed and the second maps host RateLimitExceeded into BlogGraphqlRateLimitExceeded

cases.name: disabled_memory_adapter
cases.expected: disabled host configuration remains unlimited through the Blog adapter

cases.name: http_response_header_handoff
cases.expected: the GraphQL HTTP controller preserves async-graphql response headers while retaining the JSON content type

runtime_promotion_requirements: execute the module integration target, host adapter unit target, and controller header-handoff target
execute mounted GraphQL requests against Redis-backed host composition
retain a real HTTP Retry-After response together with matching GraphQL retryAfter evidence
retain publication, channel visibility, tenant isolation, and RBAC non-regression evidence
